articles.title: Local obituary: Janet Swallow, 68, Danvers resident and longtime Lahey nurse
articles.description:
"Janet devoted her life to caring for others through her work as a nurse."

The post Local obituary: Janet Swallow, 68, Danvers resident and longtime Lahey nurse appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Janet Swallow, 68, passed away, leaving a legacy of care in Danvers.
She worked as a nurse at Lahey for many years, impacting countless patients.
Her dedication to nursing highlights the importance of healthcare professionals in the community.

articles.title: PWHL reaches TV deal with Scripps Sports to air Walter Cup Finals to national U.S. audience in May
articles.description:
“Fans shouldn’t have to wait another four years to experience this level of passion, skill and drama. So we’re keeping the momentum going.”

The post PWHL reaches TV deal with Scripps Sports to air Walter Cup Finals to national U.S. audience in May appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: PWHL partners with Scripps Sports to broadcast Walter Cup Finals in May 2024.
Boston residents can watch the finals featuring top women's hockey talent on national TV.
Increased visibility for women's hockey may boost local interest and attendance at future games.

articles.title: A decade after her father’s death, 24-year-old runs the Boston Marathon for grief support
articles.description:
Violet Morgan is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for The Children's Room.

The post A decade after her father’s death, 24-year-old runs the Boston Marathon for grief support appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Violet Morgan, 24, runs the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor her late father.
She supports The Children's Room, aiding grieving families in Boston.
Her participation raises awareness for grief support services in the community.

articles.title: WNBA and players union have reached an agreement in principle on a transformational new CBA
articles.description:
"We still need to finalize a formal term sheet, but the progress made in these discussions marks a transformative step forward for players and the league,” WNBA Commissioner Cathy Engelbert said.

The post WNBA and players union have reached an agreement in principle on a transformational new CBA appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: WNBA and players union agree on a new CBA, pending formal term sheet finalization.
Commissioner Cathy Engelbert highlights transformative progress for players and the league.
Boston fans may see improved player conditions and engagement in upcoming seasons.
